ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Will Examination
Off Calendar Sep-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This came on calendar for a forensic will examination pursuant to the court's 9-4-2015 order.

In Appearance: Albert Lee, Esq. appearing for Mr. John Reed, objector/respondent Mr. John Reed, Matthew W. S. Toth, Esq. for petitioner Gene Smith (not present), Forensic Will Examiner David S. Moore and Deputy Court Clerk Diane Hakewill

The will examination was scheduled to begin at 1:30pm in the Room 202 conference room. David Moore arrived at 2:00pm. The will examination began at 2:05pm. Mr. Moore requested that the original will be scanned onto his flashdrive at 691 dpi. This request was not included in the court order; therefore, the will was not scanned.

Mr. Moore proceeded to examine the will on site with the above-named in appearance. He performed the following tasks on the six page original will: Holding the will up to the light, comparing edges of the document, comparing the original will, including signatures, to the copy, and comparing each sheet to another within the will. During the examination he requested that the lights be lowered. The lights were not lowered, and he used a phone flashlight to examine parts of the will. Thoughout the will examination, he took approximately six pages of notes.

The Deputy Court Clerk, Ms. Hakewill, was present during the entire will examination. When she left the conference room to make inquiries, she had the original and the copy of the will in her possession at all times. Mr. Moore was provided with a copy of the original will. The will examination ended at 3:00pm.

The court stated that Mr. Reed is not present, that there are competing petitions with competing versions of wills and that there are contests to the wills and objections to the contests. Mr. Walker stated that Mr. Toth has made an effort to discuss the case with Mr. Reed but had not been successful and that they are amenable to mediation. The court stated that if the parties are unable to resolve the issues an evidentiary hearing will be necessary, that the parties need to talk about a resolution so that a schedule can be set and that if Mr. Reed wants to proceed he must appear in court and participate. The petition for letters of administration and the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary were continued to April 15, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to provide time for the parties to meet and confer and appear in court with a schedule.
